title: LATAM Healthcare Claims Analytics Gap
problem: Healthcare claims data in LATAM is underutilized. Payers have mountains of claims but limited analytics capabilities. No real-time fraud detection. No provider performance benchmarking. No cost trend analysis. No predictive modeling for high-cost patients. This leads to waste, fraud, and poor network management.
solution: Claims analytics platform for LATAM payers. Real-time fraud detection using ML. Provider performance dashboards. Cost trend analysis and forecasting. High-risk patient identification. Network adequacy analysis. Integration with payer claims systems. Custom reporting.
technology: ML fraud detection, data warehouse, visualization dashboards, predictive modeling, API integration, anomaly detection
target_customer: Health insurers, self-insured employers, EPS entities (Colombia), Ministry of Health (public payers)
ai_type: Fraud detection ML, predictive modeling for costs
traction_signals: Healthcare fraud estimated at 5-10% of spending
Proven models (CODON, Health Catalyst in US)
regulatory_notes: Data privacy regulations, healthcare fraud laws
varden_fit: HIGH FIT - Claims analytics is pure B2B infrastructure. High willingness to pay (fraud savings are immediate). Defensible through data assets. Recurring revenue.
notes: Claims analytics is sophisticated but valuable. Fraud detection alone can pay for the platform. Payer customers have budget and need.
